caption: At a rural festival, a large brown bull has escaped its pen — broken wooden fencing visible in the background — and is mid-charge across an open dirt area with head lowered and dust kicking up; three people are running away in the foreground, while festival booths and a crowd of bystanders are visible further back, most unaware of the situation.
annotator_notes: Image matches prompt. CHARGING STATE TEST — animal threat in active motion. Bull is `charging` (not `rabid` — bull is escaped not sick). Schema fix: bystander_1/person re-id'd to person_4/person_5. Distance rule: bull is actively charging at the three foreground fleeing people; distant bystanders are well behind it and not in its immediate path — direct bull->bystander edges dropped. Restored state synonyms: person_1, person_2, person_3 fleeing->running_away (all three visibly sprinting away from the charging bull in the foreground). C13 fix: added worsens self-loop(s) to standing hazard(s) without external targets (fence_1).
